observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. Acting City General Services Officer and City Accountant to reconcile their
respective property records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE.
Should reconciliation of the RPCPPE and accounting records appeared to be
improbable, consider availing the one-time cleansing of PPE in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;

b. City Accountant to propose a plan to locate the source documents and old
records of PPE items amounting to P44,263,297.57 to facilitate the preparation of
complete subsidiary records; and

c. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete annual physical count of all
properties of the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. CGSO and CAO to reconcile the balances of their respective records of
Inventories;

b. Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of all inventory items;
and

c. CGSO to prepare the SSMI for the issued inventories totaling P13,206,540.80 and
submit to the CAO for the recognition of expense using prior period adjustment.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare the JV for
the restoration of cash equivalent of the unreleased checks and recognition of the
appropriate payable/liability accounts at the end of the year and in addition, reverse
the entries made in the ensuing year to fairly present the balances of the affected
accounts in the Statement of Financial Position (SFPo).

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:

a. submit to the Audit Team all necessary documents such as Delivery Receipts,
Contractor’s Statement of Work Accomplished, Certificate of Inspection and
Acceptance and other pertinent documents to prove the validity of the recorded
Accounts Payable, otherwise make the necessary adjustments in the books of
accounts to fairly present the balances of the affected accounts in the financial
statements; and

b. refrain from recording Accounts Payable merely on the basis of obligations in
Certification on Appropriation, Funds and Obligation of Allotment, and instead,
ensure that all relevant supporting documents are attached to the claims.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Health Officer to submit promptly all the necessary documents to the City
Accounting Office to facilitate the timely recording of the receipts and utilization of
donations in kind; and

b. City Accounting Office to record all receipts of donations in kind and its subsequent
utilization in the books of accounts pursuant to pertinent provisions of the MNGAS for
LGUs.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Treasurer to thoroughly and regularly review all entries in the Abstract of
Collections and cashbooks of all Accountable Officers, particularly the
completeness of the recording of Official Receipts and for any unusual entries
therein;

b. City Accountant to institute stricter control measures to ensure that all reports
and accounting entries are correctly recorded and promptly reviewed by his
Office, particularly as to their compliance with the generally accepted accounting
rules and regulations and documentation requirements; and

c. City Accountant and City Treasurer to consider resorting to Interbank Fund
Transfer arrangement in transferring funds within the same fund classification to
avoid needless debits to Other Receivables account.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the current and non-current balances of the
Loans Payable-Domestic account and thereafter, closely monitor the balances of the
said account to ensure its fair presentation in the SFPo.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer and other concerned
officials to prepare and submit the necessary requirements to enable the City to
include in its automated payroll system the payment of the wages of its Job Order
workers.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineering Office to conduct
preliminary inspection and submit punch-lists to contractors on infrastructure
projects when they reach an accomplishment of ninety-five percent (95%) of the total
contract amount to determine defects and thus, make necessary corrections, before
proper turnover and acceptance of the projects to the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and Development
Coordinator, City Engineer, Bids and Awards Committee-Infrastructure Projects and
other concerned officials to prepare a catch-up plan to fast-track the completion of all
delayed projects and thereafter consider the conduct of early procurement activities
and ensure that all issues that may affect the implementation of a project are
discussed and resolved prior to the projects’ inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Engineer to formulate and implement an action plan to fast-track the
completion of the remaining one project amounting to P9,986,184.95 and
thereafter, ensure strict compliance with the guidelines on the issuance of
suspension order provided under the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid
unnecessary delay in the implementation of infrastructure projects;

b. City Engineer and City Planning and Development Coordinator to ensure that
necessary detailed engineering activities are conducted prior to procurement of
infrastructure projects; and

c. BAC to require the submission of certification from the CEO that detailed
engineering surveys and designs have been conducted by the latter.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City DRRMC to:

a. identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP the projects
and activities to be charged out of the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002; and

b. submit to the Audit Team a resolution enacted and approved by the Sanggunian
Panglunsod and City Mayor authorizing the use of unexpended balance of prior
year’s LDRRMF in the STF as recommended by the CDRRMO to avoid
suspension, which may result to disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to cause the
submission of the noted lacking documents to the COA Audit Team to facilitate
further technical review of the COA Technical Audit Specialist in compliance with
COA Circular No. 2009-001, else the transactions covered by the same will be
suspended in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Officer, the General Service Officer and other responsible officials to
explain in writing, for further evaluation of the Audit Team, why the Communication
Equipment Set has not been operational or used for three years and the specific
actions taken and to be taken by them to ensure that the amount of P9,985,000.00 is
not wasted, otherwise a Notice of Disallowance will be issued accordingly.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Accountant to ensure that payments
are made directly to payee/supplier through the issuance of checks, except those
expressly allowed to be paid through cash advances under the aforementioned
Circular, to safeguard funds from loss or misuse and ensure that appropriate taxes
are withheld by the City as withholding agent of the government.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to require the
contractor to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a
commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued
by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission and
confirmed by the City, otherwise any amount of advance payments that may not be
recovered by the City shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Legal Officer to initiate necessary legal remedy in order to enforce collection
of receivables and condonation of penalties of the subject delinquent taxpayer;
and

b. Acting City Treasurer to meticulously and judiciously review the compliance of the
City Treasurer’s Office (CTO) in implementing the City Tax Ordinances in order to
prevent misapplications which could result in the failure of the City to enforce
collection of receivables and provide tax relief to qualified taxpayers.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Engineer to submit written
explanation/justification for the noted deficiency and coordinate with the contractor to
refund the cost deficiency of P64,697.53, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be
issued by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-Human Resource Management
Officer to institute necessary controls to prevent or detect overlapping contracts of
Job Order personnel and similar names in two or more monthly payrolls.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed, to direct the: 
a. Inventory Committee to coordinate with the Assessor and Engineering Offices to establish the cost of each component of the road network and in the absence of records, have the Appraisal Committee appraise the current value of each component of the road network to facilitate the complete recording of the Infrastructure Asset-Road Network account, the preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of Road Network in the prescribed format and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements; 
b. General Services Officer and City Accountant to reconcile their respective property records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE; and 
c. City Accountant to record Adjustment on the disposal of unserviceable properties totaling P2,791,808.30.
observations.managementAction: The GSO had coordinated with the City Engineering Office and had been furnished with copies of Quarterly Report of on-going infrastructure projects. Nonetheless, the GSO was tasked to create an Inventory Committee that will conduct one-time cleansing.
observations.reason: Actual Physical Count of PPE was not undertaken by the GSO awaiting for one time cleansing of PPE. City Accounting Office has no complete records of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. CGSO and CAO to reconcile the balances of their respective records of Inventories;

b. Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of all inventory items; and

c. CAO to record the purchases of supplies and materials to inventory accounts instead of outright expense and comply with the perpetual inventory system in maintaining inventory records to properly account for additions to and subtractions from inventory; and
d. CGSO to prepare the RSMI for the issued inventories totaling P788,890.00 and submit to the CAO for the recognition of expense using prior period adjustment.
observations.managementAction: CGSO conducted partial physical count of inventory items. CGSO and CAO had already reconciled the balances of inventories for CY 2021. RSMI was already prepared by the CGSO for the issued inventories.
observations.reason: Record of balances of inventories between the CGSO and CAO for the current year is not reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to prepare a schedule of unreleased checks as of year-end and submit it to the Accounting Office who, on the basis thereof, shall prepare the JV for the restoration of cash equivalent of the unreleased checks and recognition of the appropriate payable/liability accounts and in addition, reverse the entries made in the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office prepared and submitted adjusting entry based on the Schedule of Unreleased Checks comprising of 110 checks with a total amount of P2,332,973.10. Verification disclosed, however, that 251 checks totaling P39,251,805.14 were unreleased as of December 31, 2022. Hence, no JV was prepared by the City Accounting Office to restore the remaining 141 checks totaling P36,918,832.04 to its respective Cash in Bank and liability accounts.
observations.reason: The City Accounting Office prepared adjusting entry based on an incomplete schedule (110 checks) thus fell short of 141 unreleased checks

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to immediately prepare adjusting journal entries for all reconciling items identified during the process of bank reconciliation to present the actual balances of accounts as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant had prepared the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the actual balances of accounts as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments on Cash in Bank-LCCA account to ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements and to maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for each of the HCIs to facilitate detailed recording and monitoring of the funds at any given time.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant had prepared the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Engineer, City Planning and Development Coordinator and other concerned officials to prepare a catch-up plan on projects that had been stalled or delayed. Thereafter, immediately resolve the cause of delays and expedite the implementation of the projects of the 20 per cent Development Fund; and
b. City Development Council and City Planning and Development Office to review the unimplemented projects funded by the continuing appropriation and determine whether they are still feasible and among the priority projects of the City. Otherwise, it is recommended that these projects be re-programmed together with the approved unexpended balances of the completed projects for the other development projects, subject to the concurrence of the Sanggunian and compliance with other existing budgeting rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: No Management action was taken.
observations.reason: Catch-up plan on the stalled/delayed projects was not prepared/submitted and the reason for delays was not explained by the concerned City officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City DRRMC to:
a. identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; and
b. submit to the Audit Team a resolution enacted and approved by the Sanggunian Panglunsod and City Mayor authorizing the use of unexpended balance of prior year’s LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund (STF) as recommended by the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (CDRRMO) to avoid suspension, which may result to disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: No Management action was taken.
observations.reason: The Unexpended balance of prior years under the Special Trust Fund was not programmed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Accountant and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit to the Audit Team for further evaluation the lacking documents pertaining to expenditures with total amount of P11,233,905.20 and P3,948,964.80 as shown in Appendices 11 and 14, respectively, to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit; and

b. concerned City Officials to adhere strictly to Section 93 of PD No. 1445 which requires government agencies to make direct payments to suppliers and ensure that applicable taxes due from government money payments to suppliers/creditors are withheld pursuant to BIR RM No. 23-2014; and

c. Acting City Treasurer, City Social Welfare and Development Officer and City Accountant to submit proofs that the 32 beneficiaries showing no signatures in the payroll received the benefits totaling P88,000.00, together with proof of refund of the two unclaimed benefits otherwise refund the said amount.
observations.managementAction: The City made direct payments to suppliers and several cash advances were made by the City to officials and employees for payment to suppliers. Photocopies of payrolls which included the names of the 32 beneficiaries showing the signatures of the beneficiaries showing the signatures of the 30 beneficiaries were submitted by the City Treasurer’s Office (CTO).
observations.reason: Several Cash advances were made by the City to officials and employees for payment to suppliers and payments to suppliers were not withheld of applicable taxes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Accountant in coordination with the Acting City Treasurer and City Social Welfare and Development Officer to submit to the Audit Team for further evaluation the photocopies of identification cards with specimen signatures or valid justification on why the documents could not be produced, otherwise the transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit; and

b. Acting City Treasurer, City Social Welfare and Development Officer and City Accountant to submit proofs that the 32 beneficiaries showing no signatures in the payroll received the benefits totaling P88,000.00, together with proof of refund of the two unclaimed benefits otherwise refund the said amount.
observations.managementAction: Photocopies of the payrolls, which included the names of the 32 beneficiaries showing the signatures of the 30 beneficiaries showing the signatures of the 30 beneficiaries received the benefits were submitted to the Audit Team. Proof of refund for two unclaimed benefits were submitted by the City Treasurer’s Office (CTO).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned City officials and the City Accountant to justify the necessity of payments, correct the deficiencies and submit to the COA Audit Team all lacking supporting documents pertinent to the subject transactions, otherwise the payments will be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: A valid Justification was submitted by the City Administrator and City Legal Officer and found reasonable and justifiable. Photocopies of the payrolls and proof of refund of the two unclaimed benefits were submitted by the City Treasurer’s Office (CTO).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to return to the Bureau of Treasury the fund transfers from various NGAs that had been dormant for more than five years or the purpose of the fund will still be used by the concerned office.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office had returned a total amount of P6,140,543.81 to the Bureau of Treasury. The amount of P3,279,753.34 was pending confirmation/evaluation as to whether or not the fund will still be used by the concerned office. The amount of P16,328.00 was with check already and ready for return to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: The amount of P3,279,753.34 was pending confirmation/evaluation and not yet returned to the Bureau of Treasury at time of report.

City Engineering Office to coordinate with the City Planning and Development Office to ensure that detailed engineering such as survey and site inspection/ investigation of the location of the proposed projects to be funded out of the 20 per cent Development Fund are carried out sufficiently before the preparation of the plan as provided under Section 17.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, and

observations.recommendation:

a. The City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (CDRRMC) to identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP, the specific projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF;

observations.reason: The City utilized P28,102,002.72 or 54.37 of the total current year appropriations. Reiterated in Observation No. 7, Page No. 64, Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Accountant and concerned City Officials to submit all lacking documents ensuring that they are properly filled out with complete information, to avoid suspension of the transactions;
b. BAC and the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the Income/Business Permit and Omnibus Sworn Statement of the suppliers of food commodities for typhoon victims amounting to P1,980,000.00; and
c. BAC and BAC Secretariat to justify its inability to comply with the posting requirement of the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and approved contract, and to notify all other bidders of its recommendations as provided under the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and
c. Concerned City Officials to ensure that the Official Receipts/Sales Invoices issued by the supplier/contractor are valid and not expired.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee submitted the required Notice of Award and eligibility documents such as Mayor’s Permits and Income Tax Returns on April 13, 2022.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were submitted; others still awaited evaluation by the Audit Team; otherwise transactions subject to Audit Notice of Suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and City Treasurer to submit immediately the nine Disbursement Vouchers and five payrolls and their supporting documents amounting to P9,896,942.56 to the Audit Team. Henceforth, the City Accountant and City Treasurer should ensure that no payment shall be effected unless the DVs are duly supported with the required documents and all these DVs and payrolls along with the supporting documents are submitted to the Audit Team within the first ten days of the ensuing month, in compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts.
observations.managementAction: Nine (9) Disbursement Vouchers and five (5) payrolls together with their supporting documents were submitted to the Audit Team. The City Accountant ensured that no DVs and payrolls are paid without being submitted to the Audit Team within the deadline.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), CENRO, CENRO, and other concerned officials to take the necessary legal procedures to correct or remedy the defects on the Contract for Collection, Hauling, Transport and Disposal of Waste of the City for the period with Global Environment Management Technologies and General Services (GEMTECH), Inc. to enable the Audit Team to conduct auditorial review and request for the technical evaluation of the contract, otherwise relevant transactions will be subject to the issuance of a Notice of Suspension; and
b. CENRO, City Accountant and other City Officials concerned to submit to the Audit Team the following: i. List of dump trucks and heavy equipment of the Service Provider with complete description and specifications; ii. designated pick-up points of the pick-up points of the barangays, and location of transfer station and the hauler's DENR accredited sanitary landfill; iii. detailed breakdown of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) and the contract amount; iv. Accomplishment Reports of the Service Provider duly certified correct by the CENRO and approved by the City Mayor, Certificate of Acceptance from the CENRO and Request for Payment from the Service Provider; v. Tipping Fees for the period from January 22 to March 22, 2020; vi. Copy of measurement of the total distance in kilometers for one complete route; vii. Estimated volume in cubic meters of garbage to be hauled and the basis of such estimate for each contract; and viii. observations.managementAction: CENRO included the designated pick-up points of the barangays and the location of transfer station and the hauler’s DENR accredited sanitary landfill in the revised Contract and Agreement. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat and the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the supporting documents and justifications and ensure posting as required, and the City Mayor and City Accountant to facilitate submission of Mayor’s/Business permit and Income Tax Return of suppliers to the Audit Team within five days to avoid suspension.
observations.managementAction: BAC Secretariat and the City Accountant submitted to the Audit Team the needed documents. The BAC and the City Accountant facilitated submission. The OIC-Tourism Office submitted LSIs details and photos. Clerical error on dates of award was explained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Social Welfare and Development Office (CSWDO) to: a. Submit evidence to account for the refund made by two beneficiaries; b. Facilitate the submission to the Audit Team of payroll reports and Social Amelioration Cards for validation; c. Investigate further and provide proof to contradict allegations by beneficiaries who claimed non-receipt of assistance; and d. Coordinate with concerned Barangay officials to facilitate the submission of updated contact numbers of beneficiaries for expanded confirmation.
observations.managementAction: CSWDO submitted photos and payrolls and SACs for some beneficiaries. They facilitated submission of documents and clarified some claims. CSWDO coordinated with Barangay officials to validate and submitted updated contact numbers for beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor ensure that all locally-hired teachers are included in the list of Applicants (RQA) of the Department of Education, and that the hiring procedures, criteria and guidelines used by the City in hiring LSB teachers be submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The City discontinued the hiring of LSB teachers. The LSB did not submit criteria or guidelines used by the City in the hiring. The City assured that teachers were included in Registry of Qualified Applicants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor ensure that the GPB is prepared and uploaded in the GPB Portal, and that the GPB preparation and submission requirements are complied with, and to ensure timely submission to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office of the GPB and GAD AR for review.
observations.managementAction: GPB for CY 2022 prepared in year 2021 was uploaded in the GPB Portal Monitoring System on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Planning and Development Officer to expedite the establishment and maintenance of a comprehensive GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; b. GFPS Focal Person to assess the training needs of all officials and employees primarily involved and prioritize the inclusion of capacity building in the GPB in the coming year/s; and c. GFPS Focal Person and GFPS Technical Working Group to ensure that only programs, projects and activities that address gender issues and concerns are included in the GPB and that the other requirements for GPB preparation and submission are complied with.
observations.managementAction: Updated City Profiling System. Attended training on GAD Plan and Budget (GPB). GFPS Focal Person attended training and the City created a GAD Database and included capacity building in GPB.
observations.reason: Programs/projects were primarily for general issues; GAD profiling system was primarily used for disaster related programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to analyze and investigate the cause/s of the net deficiencies of P34,201,909.64 between the balances of the RPT/SET balances and the List of Unpaid Taxes as of December 31, 2019, and make the necessary adjustments in their records. We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to make the corresponding adjustments to the affected accounts in order to reflect the correct balances of the same in the financial statements. Henceforth, record only the basic unpaid RPT and SET Receivables and record the penalties for delinquent accounts as income in the year of collection.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant and Acting City Treasurer assured the Team that reconciliation of their respective records will be undertaken.
observations.reason: No reconciliation of accounts was undertaken. Reiterated in Observation No 3, AAR Page No.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the LSB and other concerned officials to submit to the Auditor for evaluation the basis, criteria or guidelines used by the City in the hiring of LSB teachers. Henceforth, ensure that all locally-hired teachers are included in the list found in the Registry of Qualified Applicants (RQA).
observations.managementAction: Management informed that due to the on-set of the pandemic and for humanitarian reasons the 23 JO employees were allowed to retain their positions. The City Human Resource Management Officer informed that henceforth only those included in the RQA will be hired as LSB teachers.
observations.reason: The City retained JO employees as teachers during the pandemic; hiring procedures to align with RQA not yet fully complied.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor ensure that the schedule in the preparation of the City’s GAD Plan and Budget as provided under Section 4.1.C.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, is strictly observed by all concerned City offices to facilitate the timely submission to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office of the GPB and GAD AR for review.
observations.managementAction: The City’s GAD Focal Person admitted their lapses in the preparation of GADPB. She informed that they have coordinated with the DILG for the much needed training and promised to submit the same on time in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and Budget was not prepared on time and was submitted late to DILG Provincial Office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City Planning and Development Officer to expedite the establishment and maintenance of a comprehensive GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: The City Planning and Development Officer informed that the City’s Community Based Monitoring System (CBMS) will be the basis of the GAD Database and is still being updated. However, it was partially used in the granting of aids during the pandemic.
observations.reason: GAD Database not yet established; CBMS updating was not completed due to pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
a. City Accountant and General Services Officer to consider setting a cut-off date

to establish the cost of each component of the road network. In the absence

submit the Report on the Physical Count of Road Network in the prescribed
format; and
c. City Accountant to disclose the total road network in the Notes to Financial
Statement.

Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant and General Services Officer (GSO) assured the Audit Team that
continuous reconciliation of records were being done by their respective offices. The

updated to conform with the GAM for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct

corresponding Report of the Physical Count of Inventories to the Audit Team not
later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the City

of Delinquent Taxpayers/Taxes as of December 31, 2020 and make the necessary
adjustments in their records.

Management’s Comment
The City Accountant and Acting City Treasurer assured the Team that reconciliation of
their respective records will be undertaken.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

with records of the transfer to and receipt by beneficiaries of the animals

The City Agriculturist promised to provide the City Accountant with copies of records on
the transfer and receipt of the animals to the beneficiaries under the Swine Fattening
Program and assured the Team that close monitoring of the status of the animals under

We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Engineering Office to coordinate with the City Planning and Development
Office to ensure that detailed engineering such as survey and site
inspection/investigation of the location of the proposed projects to be funded
out of the 20 per cent Development Fund are carried out sufficiently before the
preparation of the plan as provided under Section 17.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR
of RA No. 9184, to avoid delays and other issues in project implementation; and
b. City Development Council and City Planning and Development Office to review
the unimplemented projects funded by the continuing appropriation and
determine whether they are still feasible and among the priority projects of the
City. Otherwise, it is recommended that these projects be re-programmed
together with the unexpended balances of the completed projects for the other
development projects, subject to the concurrence of the Sangguniang
Panlungsod and compliance with other existing budgeting rules and
regulations.
.
Management’s comment:
The City Planning and Development Officer explained that the main cause of delayed
projects was the various restrictions imposed by the government during the pandemic.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Team believes that the main cause of delay of projects completion was the failure
to conduct detailed engineering activities as required under Annex A of the 2016 RIRR
of RA No. 9184 as most of the reasons for stoppage of work were due to right-of-way
problems, change in site or location of project and removal of obstruction. The pandemic
only aggravated the delay.

a. City Engineer and other concerned City officials to investigate and identify the
cause/s of the delay in the completion and implementation of the project
Construction of Gymnasium at proposed Trece Martires City Parks and Plaza,
and ensure that appropriate charges, penalties and sanctions are meted out to
person/s who may be found responsible and/or accountable therefor, including
those imposable against the contractor such as liquidated damages, forfeiture
of performance security and termination of projects, among others, if
warranted;
b. City Engineer and other concerned City officials to make recommendations
and proposals to ensure the successful completion of the project;
c. City Accountant to facilitate the submission of the lacking documents to the
Audit Team within five days from receipt of this Report, to prevent the
issuance of a Notice of Suspension of the payments already made to the
Contractor; and
d. City Accountant to ensure that further disbursement of funds related to the
project are net of liquidated damages and other applicable penalties imposed
under Item 8 of Annex E of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and Section 39.3 of the
same RIRR and supported with complete documents.

Management’s comment:
The City Engineer assured us that he will act on the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to coordinate
with responsible officials to facilitate the submission, to the Audit Team of the
lacking documents, within five days from receipt of this Report and/or correction
of deficiencies noted in the audit of the procurement of Infrastructure Projects to
avoid suspension and/or disallowance of the subject transactions.

Management’s comment:
The City Accountant promised to coordinate with the City Engineering Office for the
submission of the lacking documents.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct:

b. The CDRRMC to ensure that supplemental budget or any revision in the
LDRRMF budget is supported by a revised AIP/LDRRMFIP approved by the
Sanggunian Panlungsod and City Mayor and which should, thereafter, be the
basis for the release and utilization of the LDRRMF for the identified new
expenditure items; and
c. All concerned officials to ensure that all projects and programs in the
LDRRMFIP are fully implemented.

Management’s Comment:
Management assured the Audit Team of their compliance with the audit
recommendations in the ensuing year.

issued by the supplier/contractor are valid and not expired.

Management’s Comment
Management assured the Audit Team that appropriate actions will be taken on the audit
recommendations.

Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts.

Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant committed to submit immediately to the Audit Team the DVs and
payrolls together with their corresponding supporting documents.

schools where they were assigned for several years and their contract have been

DILG JC No. 01, s. 2017 on locally hired teachers the City Government of Trece Martires
discontinued the hiring of teacher for School Year (SY) 2021 and they are now hired as
Learner Support Staff as the City’s aid/assistance in the implementation of the Early
Childhood Care and Development Programs.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor ensure that the schedule in

strictly observed by all concerned City offices to facilitate the timely submission
to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office of the GPB and GAD AR for review.

Management’s Comment:

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

Inventory Committee and the City Accountant to refrain from classifying as inventory items with serviceable life of more than one year but small enough to be considered as PPE but instead require the GSO to issue the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) to persons in actual possession of these inventory items for accountability and monitoring purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 2005-002 dated April 4, 2005; and

observations.managementAction: The City Accountant and General Services Officer are now observing the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2005-002 in the recording of inventory items. Adjustments were made in the books of accounts and the RPCPPE on the discrepancies. However, reconciliation was not yet completed and variances ranging from P38,200.00 to P120,701,389.92 among the recorded PPE were still noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to revert back to the General Fund the unexpended balance of CY 2013 LDRRMF amounting to P4,205,956.76 to be made available for other social services, in conformity with Sections 21 and 5.1.13 of RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002, respectively, and to present a more reliable financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant reverted back to the General Fund the expended balance of CY 2013 LDRRMF amounting to P4,205,956.76 and to present a more reliable financial statements

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the policies and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 or the Manual on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
a. the City Agriculturist to exert effort to retrieve all documents pertaining to the distribution of the swine to beneficiaries amounting to P100,000.00 in order for the City Accountant to adjust the Breeding Stocks account in the books of the City;
b. the concerned officials to exert efforts in finding the relevant documents such as the MOA, the report of the farmers/beneficiaries regarding the death of cattle, if any, the report on the investigation conducted and the action taken by the City on the matter to support the request from relief from accountability to be filed to COA; and
c. the City Agriculturist to conduct close and regular monitoring of the status of the remaining existing cattle under the City’s Cattle’s Dispersal Program so as transfer, birth and death of animals could be fully accounted for; and
d. the City Accountant to conduct analysis of the Breeding Stock account and effect necessary adjusting entry, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The City Agriculturist was able to retrieve the documents pertaining to the Swine Dispersal Program. The City Agriculturist promised to provide the City Accountant with copies of records on the transfer and receipt of the animals to the beneficiaries under the Swine Fattening Program and assured the Team that close monitoring of the status of the animals under the Cattle Dispersal Program is now being undertaken by her Office. However, no report regarding the death of the cattle and report on the investigation conducted and the action taken by the City on the matter were found.
observations.reason: Despite retrieval of MOAs, Management was not able to locate the report regarding the death of the cattle, and report on the investigation conducted and the action taken by the City on the matter to support the request for relief from accountability.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Market Administrator and City Treasurer to strictly implement the relevant rules and regulations of the Market and Slaughterhouse Code of Trece Martires City (2001) on the imposition of fees, execution of contract of lease, cancellation of lease right and/or eviction of delinquent stallholders; and

c. City Treasurer to update the records of receivables and payments by individual stallholders to determine receivables and their outstanding balances for monitoring and collection purposes and report the same to the City Accountant for recording in the books of accounts of the City Government for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Market Administrator informed that a meeting was scheduled between the City Mayor and the vendors/stallholders but was postponed due to the pandemic. The City Treasurer prepared Contract of Lease for stallholders and forwarded the records to the City Mayor's Office for approval. The City Treasurer forwarded records to the City Accountant. However the records were still subject to verification by the City Accountant.
observations.reason: Scheduled dialogue between the City officials and the market vendors will be rescheduled when feasible. Contracts were under review and records subject for verification by the City Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the Budget Officer to provide written explanation and/or justification on why the subject expenditures amounting to P1,094,905.70 were charged against the LDRRMF despite being not related to disaster risk reduction and management activities and to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities that will strengthen its capacity to prevent and mitigate disaster risks; and the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to ensure that only expenditures directly related to disaster risk reduction and management are charged against the LDRRMF while administrative expenses will be charged instead against the General Fund appropriations of the respective City Offices.
observations.managementAction: The City Budget Officer informed that due to insufficient funds in their regular budget some non-disaster risk management activities were charged to LDRRMF. For CY 2020 no unrelated expenses were charged against the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
a. the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002; b. all concerned officials to optimize the utilization of LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities that will strengthen its capacity to prevent and mitigate disaster risks; and c. the CDRRMO to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of CDRRMF to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of Fund.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO prepared and submitted to the Auditor’s Office the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of CDRRMF for CY 2020. Implementation of PPAs for CY 2020 was partially realigned and had increased to 73 percent as expended for COVID-19 related expenditures.
observations.reason: The LDRRMFIP for CY 2020 still had no separate caption for the projects funded out of Continuing Appropriation. Certain projects were realigned and expended for COVID-19 related expenditures.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Engineering Office to coordinate with the City Planning and Development Office, to ensure that detailed engineering such as survey and site inspection/investigation of the location of the proposed projects to be funded out of the 20 per cent Development Fund are carried out sufficiently before the preparation of the plan as provided under Section 17.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to avoid delays and other issues in project implementation; and
b. City Development Council and City Planning and Development Office to review the unimplemented projects funded by the continuing appropriation and determine whether they are still feasible and among the priority projects of the City. Otherwise, these projects be re-programmed together with the unexpended balances of the completed projects for the other development projects, subject to the concurrence of the Sangguniang Panlungsod and compliance with other existing budgeting rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The City Planning and Development Officer explained that the main cause of delayed projects was the various restrictions imposed by the government during the pandemic. In CY 2019, realignment of various unimplemented projects had been effected under Resolution No. 2019-043 of the Sangguniang Panlungsod. The City Accountant refrained from over estimating the amount of loan repayments and adjusted records accordingly.
observations.reason: Coordination between the City Planning and the City Engineering Office was not undertaken resulting in delays in implementation of infrastructure projects as adequate planning and detailed engineering were not carried out before the start of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineering Office and the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Auditor the approved contracts and all the documents forming part thereof within five days from their execution as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to facilitate prompt review and early communication to Management of the deficiencies, if any.
observations.managementAction: Copies of approved infrastructure contracts were prepared and forwarded by the BAC Secretariat and the City Engineering Office to the Auditor's Office for review. Purchase Orders and contracts for procurement of goods are also required to be submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Some copies of infrastructure contracts were submitted to the Audit Team since the BAC was not aware that approved Purchase Orders and contracts for procurement of goods are also required to be submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction: In compliance with CY 2018 audit recommendation, an inventory of PPE was undertaken by the Inventory Team constituted for the purpose. However, comparison of the recorded PPE per accounting records and the submitted RPCPPE still showed unreconciled variances ranging from P74,048.00 to P26,529,944.34, as shown in detail in Appendix 6 and summarized as follows: (table omitted here). Further verification disclosed several factors that contributed to the above noted net discrepancy of P69,185,248.72. In particular, our review of the RPCPPE showed that there were several accounts or items which should not anymore be recorded or included therein following the RCALGUs. Further analysis also revealed other errors in the recording of PPE in the RPCPPE and in the books of accounts. Our audit observations are outlined hereunder: a. Tangible items with serviceable life of more than one year but small enough to be considered as PPE totaling P2,180,645.40 were included under different PPE classifications in the RPCPPE as shown in Appendix 7. The Accounting Office likewise recorded them as PPE, thus overstating the related PPE and Government Equity accounts by the amount equivalent to their carrying values totaling P608,625.75; b. Items totaling P2,981,728.39 that should have been recorded as expenses were taken up as PPE both in the RPCPPE and the books of accounts, resulting in overstatement of the related PPE and Government Equity accounts by the amount, equivalent to their carrying value totaling P304,750.34 details of which are shown in Appendix 8; c. Land in SEF totaling P20,874,400.00 was erroneously recorded as P19,674,400.00 or a difference of P1,200,000.00 in the RPCPPE and accounting records (Appendix 9); d. PPE items procured in CY 2020 totaling P10,959,770.43 were already included in the RPCPPE. The list of these PPE items are shown in Appendix 10; e. Inventory for distribution totaling P342,775.00 which have been given to intended beneficiaries were still included in the RPCPPE (Appendix 11); f. Road lots with a total cost of P14,832,100.00 were recorded in the Land account instead of Road Network in the RPCPPE (Appendix 12); g. PPE classified as Other Structures and Other Land Improvements totaling P100,673,919.50 both in the RPCPPE and the books of accounts were not reclassified to Road Network account (Appendix 13); h. Computer software installed as part of the City’s computerization program/project totaling P71,170,000.00 were recorded as part of Information and Communication Technology Equipment account instead of Intangible Assets-Computer Software account both in the RPCPPE and in the accounting records (Appendix 14). The inability of the City to classify the PPE accounts in the RPCPPE or books of accounts or both in accordance with the RCALGUs, to reconcile the variances and to correct the errors earlier discussed casted doubt on the reliability of the balances of the PPE accounts in both records. Specifically, the misclassification and errors in the books of accounts significantly affected the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements of the City.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Accountant, Treasurer and Disbursing Officer to adhere strictly to the following provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, to wit:

No additional cash advance shall be granted unless the previous cash advance is fully settled and accounted for in the books.

Cash advance from one Accountable Officer to another shall not be allowed.



Amount of cash advances for payrolls shall equal to the total payrolls for a pay period.



Cash advances shall be liquidated within the prescribed period.





Payment for petty operating expenses shall be allowed only for the amounts not exceeding P15,000.00 for each transaction, except when a higher amount is allowed by law and/or specific authority by COA. Splitting of transactions to avoid exceeding the ceiling shall not be allowed.

Officials or employees granted with cash advances for special purpose amounting to P5,000.00 or more shall be bonded.
observations.managementAction:
The practice of releasing the cash advances from the Disbursing Officer to other employees has ceased since July 2016.

The amount of excess of cash advance over disbursements for payrolls and the refunds have decreased this year.





Payments for petty operating expenses have not exceeded P15,000.00 for each transaction.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
the City Accountant to book up the 36 parcels of land which were already in the name of the City Government valued at market price of P28,945,520.00;
the General Services Officer to verify with the Register of Deeds of the Province of Cavite the correctness and validity of the TCTs referred in the 27 Tax Declarations to establish the absolute ownership of the City Government over the said parcels of land.
the City Assessor and other officials concerned to facilitate the titling of the 41 parcels of land in the name of the City Government of Trece Martires to establish  the  City’s absolute ownership thereof.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
all officers and employees concerned to immediately liquidate all outstanding cash advances and strictly observe pertinent provisions of COA Circular   No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2009-002.
the OIC-City Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of Items 6.1.5  and 6.1.8.4 of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the grant of subsequent cash advances and submission of cash advance vouchers, their covering transmittal letters and supporting documents.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Accountant and Disbursing Officer to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the In-Charge of Office (ICO)-City Treasurer to strictly enforce the remedies for the collection of delinquent Real Property Taxes by administrative action through levy on real property and sale of real property by public auction or by judicial action as provided under Section 2A.45 of The 2012 Revenue Code of Trece Martires City.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the ICO-City Treasurer and OIC-City Accountant to transfer immediately the unexpended/unobligated balances of the Quick Response Fund and the Mitigation Fund-MOOE to the Trust Liability-DRRM of the Trust Fund books, in compliance with Section 21 of RA No. 10121 and Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to ensure that the LDRRMF is utilized only for purposes for which it was intended.

We recommended that the OIC-City Accountant submit the Disbursement Vouchers/Payrolls and its supporting documents to the Commission on Audit within to avoid possible suspension/disallowance in audit.  Henceforth, ensure that all accounts are submitted within the first ten days of the ensuing month as required under Section 7.2 of the Commission on Audit RRSA.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-Accountant to submit all the required documents for garbage disposal to the Auditor  for further review and evaluation, otherwise, the payments made to the contractors may be suspended/disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Accountant to and other officials concerned to submit the lacking documents to the Commission on Audit and correct the deficiencies noted in audit to facilitate the conduct of auditorial review, technical evaluation and determination of the reasonableness of the purchase price of the subject property and  to avoid possible suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
strictly implement the provisions of Section 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and ensure that the implementation of the 20% Development Fund is in accordance with the timeline indicated in their Annual Investment Plan (AIP) so that maximum benefits are availed of by the intended beneficiaries;
require the City Development Council and City Planning and Development Coordinator to:
properly plan the programs/projects of the 20% Development Fund so as to avoid realignments and delays in the implementation of programs/projects.
review the projects enumerated in the continuing appropriation and determine whether these are still feasible and priority projects of the City, otherwise, re-program the said projects together with the unexpended balance of the completed projects with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Panlungsod, for other development projects that will address the present needs of the constituents.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Development Council and City Planning and Development Coordinator to:
submit explanation or justification, for further evaluation of the Audit Team, as to why those expenses were charged against the 20% Development Fund, otherwise, the subject transactions shall be suspended or disallowed in audit in accordance with the provision of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA); and
allocate 20% Development Fund  only to programs, projects and activities as enumerated in Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1  dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the District Supervisor and other persons liable to refund the total amount of P1,580,000.00  for the expenses incurred during the GAD sensitivity seminar-workshop and Team Building Activity.
We also recommended that the Local School Board see to it that Special Education Fund is utilized for purposes provided under Section 272 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 as implemented by DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, 01-A dated April 14, 1998 and March 14, 2000, respectively, to ensure that the purpose for which the Fund was intended is met.  Otherwise, charges of expenditures that are not included under the aforementioned provisions will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
the concerned employee assigned in the issuance of the books and the recipient-teachers of  TMCNHS and TMCES to present to the Audit Team the books  worth P1,017,885.00, not seen and accounted during the ocular inspections for validation. Otherwise, Notice of Disallowance shall be issued for books that could not be presented.
the City Administrator to ensure that purchases of books are based on the specific needs,  priorities and/or valid requests from the end-users.

We recommended that the City Mayor submit authority to purchase from the  Secretary of the Interior and Local Government (SILG), in compliance with  DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the ICO-City Treasurer and OIC-City Accountant to remit the unexpended balance of the completed projects totaling  P278,973.01 to the National Treasury/Source Agency as required under  COA Joint Circular No. 9-81 dated October 28, 1981 and Item  4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 so as the national government could appropriate them to other essential projects.
We also recommended that once the fund for the Construction of Agtas Bridge at Aguado-Cabuco Road is obtained, expedite the implementation of the same.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management Board to strictly implement the activities stated in the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan within the timeframe. Likewise, follow up the status of the case with the Office of the Ombudsman to facilitate the approval of the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Accountant to: 

coordinate with the City Treasurer’s Office to obtain the desired information on the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable; 

reconcile the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable with the records of the Treasurer’s Office; and 


maintain and update regularly the Subsidiary Ledgers.
